title: The Communist Programme
description: The Communist Programme is a pamphlet by Unknown author. Some time before May 1921, a man was sentenced to three months' hard labour for selling copies of The Communist Programme.
descriptionParagraphs: The Communist Programme is a pamphlet by Unknown author. Some time before May 1921, a man was sentenced to three months' hard labour for selling copies of The Communist Programme.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a pamphlet, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Some time before May 1921, a man was sentenced to three months' hard labour for selling copies of The Communist Programme. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: The Communist Programme is a pamphlet by Unknown author. Some time before May 1921, a man was sentenced to three months' hard labour for selling copies of The Communist Programme.
banningParagraphs: The Communist Programme entered censorship debates as a pamphlet associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 20th century in New Zealand, where New Zealand censorship authorities classified, prohibited, or restricted. Some time before May 1921, a man was sentenced to three months' hard labour for selling copies of The Communist Programme. Some time before May 1921, a man was sentenced to three months' hard labour for selling copies of The Communist Programme.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
